Regarding God’s conflict with David over sin, he prays, “Do not judge me, for if you do, I will be condemned.” But regarding the conflict his enemies had with him, he prays, “Lord, judge me and show your favor toward me.” If we cannot find comfort in God, we may rely on him and have spiritual support when we lack spiritual joy. He never rejected anyone who trusted in him, no matter what fears they had about their own condition. We need desire nothing more to make us happy than the good that comes from God’s favor and is included in his promise. Those whom God leads, he leads to his holy hill; therefore, those who claim to be led by the Spirit but turn away from established practices deceive themselves. We must still pray for the Spirit of light and truth, who compensates for the absence of Christ's physical presence, to guide us on the way to heaven. Whatever we rejoice in or celebrate, the Lord must be the source of that joy. David looks to God as his constant hope. Let us earnestly pray that the Lord would send forth the truth of his word and the light of his Spirit to guide us into the way of holiness, peace, and salvation. The desire of a Christian, like that of a prophet in distress, is to be saved from sin as well as sorrow; to be taught in the way of righteousness by the light of heavenly wisdom shining in Jesus Christ, and to be led by this light and truth to the New Jerusalem.
